channel.title: Golangs on sysrex
channel.description: Recent content in Golangs on sysrex

item.title: Parse environment variables to structs in Go
item.description: In Go, it’s dead simple to get the value from an environment variable: fmt.Println(os.Getenv("HOME")) But, sometimes you have default values… so you would have to do something like this: home := os.Getenv("HOME") if home == "" { home = "THE DEFAULT HOME" } fmt.Println(home) If you need those values in a lot of places, you would end up creating a function for each one or something like that. I found this to be extremely boring.

item.title: Go variables
item.description: Just a small overview of the go variables
